description_long: This is a modern, multi-unit bungalow complex built into a sloped terrain, featuring two main residential structures separated by a central courtyard. The buildings have flat and slightly angled roofs, large floor-to-ceiling windows, and multiple outdoor terraces with wooden decking. The property is surrounded by retaining walls and landscaped green areas, with a clear division between private and shared spaces. The front facade showcases expansive glass panels and open patios, while the rear is more enclosed and built into the hillside. The use of concrete, glass, and wood is prominent throughout the exterior, giving the complex a sleek, contemporary appearance. The layout maximizes views and natural light, with multiple entry points and outdoor living areas.
description: A modern, multi-unit bungalow complex with flat roofs, large glass windows, and terraced outdoor spaces built into a sloped landscape. The design features concrete, wood, and glass materials with landscaped retaining walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing both main buildings, central courtyard, large glass windows, and terraced patios.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stepped terrain, retaining walls, and the depth of the terraces.
description_view_2: Rear view with more enclosed facades, minimal windows, and the buildings embedded into the hillside.
description_view_3: Angled side view emphasizing the multi-level terraces, outdoor spaces, and the relationship between the two buildings.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large grouped windows and floor-to-ceiling glass panels
